summary.note: Timing/token telemetry unavailable in this environment.

eval-0 (calibrated-small-skill-tightenin.note: Both saturated. Useful for regression but adds no separation signal.

eval-2 (cli-config-validation-module-han.note: Prompt was too directive; both configs followed explicit instructions in the task prompt and produced the required checkpoints. The discrimination the analyzer recommended was achieved in the new evals 3 and 4 instead. To make eval-2 discriminating in a future iteration, the assertion should test structural placement (TDD loop inside Validation Plan section vs. Implementation Slices), not mere presence.

failures_in_baseline: code-simplifier never named as a subagent; simplicity folded into generic code-reviewer
code-simplifier not named as a dedicated subagent
sequencing assertion moot because simplifier absent

recommendations: Eval-0 and eval-2 are both saturated/non-discriminating — replace or add variants that test edge cases: e.g. eval-0 variant where TDD/code-simplifier instructions are absent from the skill, eval-2 where assertion tests structural placement of TDD loop (in Validation Plan section, not just Implementation Slices).

The eval suite now clearly discriminates on the code-simplifier subagent gap (evals 1, 3, 4). Consider a fifth discriminating eval focused on the 'do not bypass TDD' enforcement in Phase 5 under time pressure.
